trajectory_summary: This call opened at maximum customer cooperation — Cedric self-proposed the appointment within 15 seconds — and Bucky converted it cleanly into a booked sit. The trajectory is ascending throughout, with no energy leaks from resistance. The call's weaknesses are structural omissions (program_pitch skipped, bill anchor not flipped, button-up elements missing, text follow-up not promised) that don't show up as energy loss because Cedric never required them. The appointment is real but conditionally fragile: an HOA rear-placement constraint was disclosed at close, a delivery-day scheduling anchor creates split-attention risk, and the closer has no emotional context beyond 'saving money.'
outcome_dispute: The v1 tag is unknown. Based on the transcript, the outcome is a booked appointment (March 27, noon) with a qualified homeowner. The appointment carries conditional fragility — HOA placement constraint, delivery-day split attention, single-month ownership with no bill history — but it is a legitimate sit. Tag should read: booked_conditional.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Button-up completeness and text follow-up discipline
primary_coaching_focus.quote: I'm gonna get your design built, and then we will see you on the twenty seventh at noon.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Bucky hit one of six required button-up elements on a booked appointment with a conditionally fragile sit (delivery day, HOA constraint, traveling customer). The missing 'is that fair?' handshake, computer/no-driving anchor, and text follow-up with tieback are show-rate predictors — their absence is a direct no-show risk on a sit Bucky otherwise earned cleanly. This is the highest-leverage fix because it applies to every call Bucky books, not just this one.

key_moments.quote: What's your interest in solar? Just saving money?
key_moments.stewart_read: Bucky leads the answer — 'just saving money?' — converting the codex's most important emotional anchor question into a yes/no checkbox. Cedric confirms ('Yes. And, like, Allstate. You know?') and adds an insurance angle that Bucky doesn't probe. The bill anchor is captured as a data point but never flipped into emotional alignment-against-the-utility. Reactive read holds: this is a missed depth moment, not a strategic deferral — there's no later move that picks it up.

Bucky notes HOA presence on the record — apparently from the satellite map view — but never asks Cedric whether the HOA has solar restrictions. The rear-placement constraint Cedric volunteers at 07:40 ('it has to be on the rear of the house, it cannot be seen from the street') is a direct HOA rule. Bucky had the signal at 01:37 and walked past it. The constraint was foreseeable, not a surprise. Reactive read holds: this is an early signal missed, not a strategic deferral.

key_moments.quote: I can tell you what my kilowatt usage is right now.
key_moments.stewart_read: Cedric has the Dominion app open and is ready to share live usage data — he's not just cooperative, he's technically engaged and motivated enough to pull the data in real time. This is a qualification-positive signal: a new homeowner who immediately opens his utility app on a sales call is a high-intent lead. Bucky accepts the data but doesn't anchor on the enthusiasm — he treats it as a logistics problem (get the photo) rather than a trust-building moment.

key_moments.quote: Okay. Let me just go talk to him, and then I think we'll we'll be good to go.
key_moments.stewart_read: Bucky puts Cedric on hold to get supervisor approval on whether a single current-day kWh reading (182 kWh) is sufficient to proceed. This reveals he doesn't know the internal threshold for what constitutes an approvable data point on a new-homeowner account. The hold adds dead air and mild friction to a call that had been frictionless. Reactive read holds: this is a genuine knowledge gap, not a strategic pause.
